Framework

What Changes. What Doesn't.

01

The Equations Stay

General Relativity, Quantum Mechanics, and the Standard Model remain intact. WST does not replace their mathematics — it reveals the geometric picture those equations describe.

02

Particles Become Waves

The electron is a Möbius-twisted torus. The proton is a trefoil knot. The photon is an open helix. The four forces are four geometric regimes of one substrate field.

03

Constants Become Consequences

The numbers at the foundation of physics are not arbitrary inputs. They are consequences of shape — ratios that emerge inevitably from the wave geometry of matter.

The Book

A Geometric Foundation
for Physics

Thirteen derived quantities. Two structural predictions.
One novel mechanism. Zero free parameters.

Wave-Substrate Theory proposes that matter and forces emerge from stable standing-wave structures of a continuous substrate. The familiar equations of physics remain correct; what changes is the picture underneath.

The book derives fifteen fundamental constants from geometry alone — numbers that have been measured to extraordinary precision but never explained. The fine-structure constant. Mass ratios. Mixing angles. The values that define how atoms hold together, how light behaves, and how matter exists at all.

No fitting. No tuning. No adjustable parameters. Every derivation is shown. Every prediction is falsifiable.

This is Book One of three. Volume II extends the framework to chemistry. Volume III to biology.
